title: Barriers To Reporting Workplace Violence Include
source_filename: Barriers To Reporting Workplace Violence Include.docx

qas.q: Health care workers in inpatient facilities experienced workplace violence at least __________ higher than the rate of private sector workers overall.
qas.a: 5-12 times

qas.q: About only ______ percent of nurses report incidents of violence.

qas.a:
- a health care culture that considers workplace violence as part of the job
- a perception that violent incidents are routine
- lack of agreement on the definition of violence
- fear of being of being accused of inadequate performance
- lack of awareness of the reporting system
- a belief that reporting will not change anything
- a practice of not reporting unintentional violence
- lack of manager/employee support
- lack of training to reporting workplace violence
- a fear of reporting supervisory workplace violence
Just Culture
culture of safety
Leading a Culture of Safety: A Blueprint for Success
sentinel event
Incivility, Bullying and Workplace Violence

qas.q: _______ is one of the biggest barriers towards reducing workplace violence
qas.a: lack of reporting
primary, secondary, tertiary
stopping violence before it occurs. develop education and other strategies to identify risk, increase buffers and reduce vulnerabilities in order to prevent workplace violence
immediate and effective response to violence including emergency care. intervention intended to address emergent short term consequences and reduce the negative impact of workplace violence
long term responses to violence. intervention intended to reduce the long-term negative and rehabilitation consequences of workplace violence
true
polite and courteous behavior
communication that is rude, impolite, discourteous, and lacking in good manners
incivility beyond impoliteness

lateral
vertical
vicious
speech/action disrupting harmony in teaching/learning

stress
rapid change

dont eat your young
mentoring
positivity

some state laws
2016 TJC guidelines
ANA code of ethics
professional standards, codes of conduct

workplace hostility
bullying
lateral violence
horizontal violence
vertical violence
workplace violence
the threat of violence or the actual causing of physical harm to workers either inside or outside the workplace

qas.q: applying ____ can be helpful in reducing incivility
qas.a: QSEN

qas.q: Keys to prevent workplace violence include
qas.a:
talking about problems
not participating in incivility
self care

qas.q: __________ is needed to end workplace incivility
qas.a: leadership
positive state of health that is continually changing
Physical manifestations and subjective experiences
functional or structural disturbance
A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being; is not merely an absence of disease

qas.q: shift from treatment if illnesses to....
qas.a: health promotion (focus on primary prevention)

-age
-current health status
-presence of risk factors
-health preferences and values
-social relationship
the process of enabling people to increase control over and improve their health

individual
family

parietal roles

work with people to enhance health

qas.q: which statement best describes the relationship between caring and civility in nursing
qas.a: Engaging in civil behavior is a way to demonstrate caring for a client coworkers and others

qas.q: A nurse manager is concerned about instability on her unit. The nurses on dayshift gossip about the night nurses unlicensed assistive personnel complain that the nurses refused to help them turn morbidly obese clients and during staff meetings everyone looks at their phones. When the nurse manager shared her concerns with the supervisor she identifies the specific problem as___.
qas.a: lateral violence

qas.q: Which statement about incivility in the educational setting is true? Select all that apply.
qas.a:
incivility in the educational setting may involve administrators faculty and students
Instability among nursing educators is sometimes related to differences in educational backgrounds and professional preparation
There is a correlation between increase student stress and student and civility

qas.q: students in Dr. Reynolds nursing fundamentals class routinely leave scathing reviews of him on the professor rating website refused to participate in class activities and complain to the dean about how strict Dr. Reynolds is. What activity are the students engaged in?
qas.a: Academic incivility

qas.q: Rodney a new RN just started training on a busy medical surgical unit. He has been warned that the experience nurses on the unit will probably expect him to pay his dues just as they had to. This type of initiation practice is commonly referred to as___.
qas.a: nurses eat their young

qas.q: Mara is reviewing her class notes on instability in nursing ethics. Which of the following notes should she request clarification about?
qas.a: The a N a code of ethics mentions extending respect to all encounters except for colleagues

qas.q: Which of the following behaviors would be considered civil in both the classroom and clinical setting? Select all that apply.
qas.a:
assume Goodwill
listen to and respect others
be on time

qas.q: kerell is listing examples of civil classroom behavior for the nursing students in her study group. Which examples of the other members of the study group question?
qas.a: Play computer games quietly so as not to disturb others

qas.q: which of the following behaviors are characteristic of both bullying and lateral violence? Select all that apply.
qas.a:
can escalate to physical actions
intimidation
name calling
verbal sarcasm
use of social media to attack target

qas.q: What is the main difference between bullying and lateral violence?
qas.a: Lateral violence occurs primarily in the work setting
workplace bullying
Bullying from a superior, or vertical violence, is a type of harassment that can permeate the entire organization and have a detrimental effect on its effectiveness.
type of vertical violenece; any quality of power between the experienced nurse or faculty member and the new nurse or nursing student.
